title: Rice Command Center - Multi-Surface Demo Mode Pilot
stack: Next.js web app + /digest PWA, Chrome extension overlay, Node/Express API, Google OAuth/Calendar, n8n (demoted orchestration)
summary: Built a multi-surface executive assistant pilot with Live Data/Demo Mode, guided storyline, auditability, and read-only safety guardrails.
outcomes: Multi-surface delivery shipped (web app + PWA + extension)
Pilot APIs, RBAC-lite, and audit export implemented
Demo mode is clearly labeled and no write-back is allowed

useCase: This is a multi-surface web product: primary web app in Next.js, mobile PWA view at /digest on iPhone, Chrome extension overlay companion, and Node/Express backend APIs.
It is not a native iPhone app in the current phase; mobile delivery is web plus PWA.

Demo Mode design: top-bar Live Data / Demo Mode toggle, first-open guided demo, scripted storyline (morning brief, updates, meeting prep, action center, EA triage), timed alert cards in /digest, optional local notifications for installed PWA, explicit Demo Data and Draft/Concept truth labels, and read-only behavior.

statusMatrix.currentState: OAuth start/callback/me/logout live; domain allowlist enforced; backend owns tokens.
statusMatrix.next: Add admin UI for allowed domains and env validation.

statusMatrix.currentState: OAUTH_TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY required; tokens encrypted at rest.
statusMatrix.next: Move key to managed secret store for cloud environments.

statusMatrix.currentState: Google Calendar read works; fallback to demo dataset if API is unavailable.
statusMatrix.next: Add better calendar error telemetry in UI.

statusMatrix.currentState: Meetings, prep context, generated summary, decisions, and follow-ups are rendered.
statusMatrix.next: Tighten ranking and wording for decision quality.

statusMatrix.currentState: Deterministic checks implemented for meeting quality and conflicts.
statusMatrix.next: Add per-rule tuning from pilot feedback.

statusMatrix.currentState: Availability and conflict suggestions with rationale and draft-only actions in UI.
statusMatrix.next: Add explicit export draft email action.

statusMatrix.currentState: /api/attention-queue implemented and used in Overview and Meeting Mode.
statusMatrix.next: Add personalization weights from onboarding preferences.

statusMatrix.currentState: /api/morning-digest and /digest shipped; auto-refresh every five minutes in working hours.
statusMatrix.next: Add push notification layer if still in scope.

statusMatrix.currentState: UX exists; live transcript is simulated and clearly not production capture.
statusMatrix.next: Integrate real transcript stream provider.

statusMatrix.currentState: /api/email/ingest supports label/forwarded modes, retention, audit event, and artifact storage.
statusMatrix.next: Add UI controls for ingest mode and retention summary.

statusMatrix.currentState: /api/onboarding GET/POST exists for VIPs, urgent phrases, delegate rules, and preferences.
statusMatrix.next: Build onboarding UI screen.

statusMatrix.currentState: Backend fetches Google data; n8n is used for orchestration and repair loops only.
statusMatrix.next: Add n8n workflow health dashboard.

statusMatrix.currentState: Floating overlay injects on all pages, compact iframe mode, and open full app action.
statusMatrix.next: Add performance guardrails and optional domain allowlist for injection.

statusMatrix.currentState: Correlation IDs, request logging, rate limiting, and Google retry/backoff are in place.
statusMatrix.next: Add centralized log sink and alerts.

statusMatrix.currentState: IDataStore plus JsonDataStore wired; API decoupled from direct db.ts imports.
statusMatrix.next: Implement PostgresDataStore adapter.

statusMatrix.currentState: Contract tests exist and pass.
statusMatrix.next: Add integration tests and key UI end-to-end flows.

statusMatrix.currentState: Current mobile delivery is responsive web plus PWA route (/digest), not native iOS.
statusMatrix.next: Decide whether native iOS is a separate phase.

implementationPlan: Add pilot APIs: doc-finder, meeting-prep, gmail/sync (incremental), executive-pulse, and audit.
Keep OAuth and token ownership in backend; keep n8n demoted and out of token lifecycle.
Enforce pilot RBAC-lite roles (executive, ea, viewer), with viewer limited to audit export.
Add IDataStore methods for email sync cursor/state and audit querying to preserve clean Postgres migration path.
Implement Document Finder v1 with Drive folder scope and demo fallback.
Implement incremental Gmail metadata sync with processed-ID cursor persistence.
Update Executive Pulse for morning versus during-day with classification, action, and source traceability.
Add UI pilot-scope labels (Implemented, Draft, Concept) and wire doc suggestions into meeting prep surfaces.
Upgrade demo dataset with Rice public source-backed document evidence.
Keep strict schema validation, tests, and build pipeline green.
implementationHighlights: types.ts, schemas.ts, examples.ts, validators.ts: new contract types, schemas, and validators for doc-finder, meeting-prep, executive-pulse, and audit.
demoData.ts and demo-dataset.json: expanded documents and richer public source evidence.
config.ts and .env.example: provider config for document finder and RBAC-lite role settings.
googleAuth.ts: Drive client support for scoped document search.
IDataStore.ts, JsonDataStore.ts, db.ts: email sync state and audit query persistence methods.
New services: docFinder.ts, meetingPrep.ts, gmailSync.ts, executivePulse.ts, and rbac.ts.
morningDigest.ts: now prefers stored artifacts and emits classification plus time-energy buckets.
index.ts: new endpoints, RBAC-lite enforcement, leader access checks, and audit logging.
contracts.test.ts plus docs (api-contracts, pilot-scope, security-notes, smoke-tests) updated for pilot scope.
testChecklist: Open http://localhost:3000 and verify Executive Pulse shows morning and during-day items with classification and source chips.
Use Calendar and Prep: select a meeting, search docs, and verify suggested docs and source links.
Use Prepare Next Meeting panel and confirm suggested docs plus why-it-matters text.
Trigger Export audit and verify JSON export works through pilot viewer path.
knownLimitations: No write-back execution: all actions are draft-only.
Document Finder is Drive-folder scoped; if unavailable it falls back to demo docs and may require review.
Gmail ingest and sync stores metadata artifacts only, not full email body by default.
RBAC is pilot-lite (env allowlists plus optional role override), not enterprise IAM.
Box, OneDrive, and SharePoint connectors are not implemented in pilot phase.

title: Laura AI Agent - Multi-Channel RAG Workflow
stack: WordPress embedded chat, API Gateway/Webhooks, n8n orchestration, RAG over SOP corpus, LLM reasoning and response controls
summary: Designed an end-to-end AI agent workflow for Web, WhatsApp, and Voice channels using retrieval-grounded responses and a security-first gateway.
outcomes: Replaced static pricing logic with dynamic SOP-grounded answers
Unified intake for Web, WhatsApp, and Voice user channels
Security controls enforced before any LLM processing

useCase: Automation starts from a client-facing Web Chat interface embedded in the company WordPress portal.
To ensure pricing and service accuracy, the workflow uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation instead of hardcoded static values.
The agent dynamically queries Standard Operating Procedures (SOP) to produce precise, policy-aligned responses.

statusMatrix.currentState: Defined Start phase and multi-channel entry points (Web, WhatsApp, Voice).
statusMatrix.next: Implement channel adapters and unified intake validation.

statusMatrix.currentState: Defined receive, credential validation, rate limiting, sensitive-data masking, and security decision checks.
statusMatrix.next: Implement webhook gateway policies and threat logging.

statusMatrix.currentState: Defined session context creation/loading as the first step in conversation intelligence.
statusMatrix.next: Add language detection and intent routing.

statusMatrix.currentState: Defined retrieval stage to query current SOP/pricing documentation.
statusMatrix.next: Connect indexed document store and freshness controls.

statusMatrix.currentState: Defined generation stage driven by retrieved evidence and business constraints.
statusMatrix.next: Add output schema validation and response guardrails.

statusMatrix.currentState: Defined operational handoff surfaces in architecture.
statusMatrix.next: Implement action logging and operator approvals.

statusMatrix.currentState: Defined integration lane for downstream enterprise process execution.
statusMatrix.next: Connect selected RPA/API targets and retry strategy.

statusMatrix.currentState: Defined decision logging path for errors and threat events.
statusMatrix.next: Add observability dashboards and retention policies.

implementationPlan: Implement a single intake contract for Web, WhatsApp, and Voice channels.
Enforce API Gateway security checks before message processing.
Create session context and conversation state persistence.
Integrate SOP document retrieval as the primary source of truth.
Generate responses with strict schema validation and confidence checks.
Add business console actions and human-in-the-loop controls.
Wire downstream execution through approved RPA and API connectors.
Ship observability, audit logs, and anti-abuse controls as first-class features.
knownLimitations: Current definition is architecture and process design, not a full production deployment snapshot.
RAG quality depends on SOP indexing freshness and content governance.
Voice/WhatsApp adapters require provider-specific implementation and compliance review.
